name: Technical Feasibility

validated_findings.finding: Authentication, RBAC, file storage, audit logging, OCR, translation, and exchange-rate display are technically feasible using established service patterns.

evidence.summary: Stage 1 decomposes these areas into discrete dependencies with workflow support mappings.

validated_findings.finding: External crowdfunding or payment-provider delegation is technically feasible for MVP and removes Crewly-owned money movement from the launch architecture.

evidence.summary: Connect supports platforms and marketplaces that manage payments and move money between multiple parties.
Stripe documents direct charges, destination charges, and separate charges and transfers, showing why Crewly should avoid owning funds flow in MVP when using an external-provider strategy.
User selected Option 3: use an external crowdfunding/payment provider instead of Crewly-owned pooled payments for MVP.

resolved_unknowns.description: The compliant payment model for organizer-controlled pooled trip support is resolved for MVP by delegating checkout, KYC, refunds, disputes, and payouts to an external crowdfunding/payment provider.
resolved_unknowns.next_action: Stage 3 should model an ExternalContributionProvider boundary with provider URL/linking, contribution status import, manual reconciliation, and webhook/export adapter options.

technical_constraints_for_stage_3: Use separate ledger domains for trip funds, trip expenses, and private event expenses.
Preserve original receipt values as financial source of truth; translated text and converted amounts are display aids.
Require audit logs for payment, receipt, ledger, event, and visibility changes.
Model public contributions through an ExternalContributionProvider boundary; Crewly must not process, hold, refund, dispute, or pay out funds directly in MVP.
